pre_game.message: Hey, I'm G. This whole setup is pretty wild, isn't it? Are you feeling ready for whatever they're about to throw at us?
Hey G, I'm D. "Wild" is an understatement. I'm ready because I have to be—there’s a lot riding on this for me. How are you holding up?
I’m feeling focused. I love a good game, but with my family counting on me, the stakes feel huge. I’m definitely here to win—what’s your main motivation?
I took a massive hit in the market yesterday—lost twenty grand. I'm here to make that back so I can take care of my people and get back on my feet.
That’s a heavy blow, D. I’m sorry to hear that, but it sounds like we’re both playing for high stakes today—I'm definitely bringing my A-game.
I respect that. When things get this real, you have to be at your best. Let's see what we're up against.
Definitely. I'm just trying to read the room now—it's hard to tell who's going to play fair and who's going to be cutthroat. What's your gut telling you about the others?
My gut says everyone’s probably as desperate as we are, and desperation makes people unpredictable. I’m keeping my guard up until we see how this plays out.
Smart move. Desperation definitely changes the game. I'm keeping my eyes open too, but I'm hoping we can find a way to win without things getting too ugly.
I hope so too, G. I'd prefer a clean win, but I'm not leaving here empty-handed. Good luck out there.
I hear you loud and clear. Let's hope the game gives us a path where we can both walk away winners. See you in there.

post_game.message: I'm sorry you only got $625. I tried to keep things fair by taking exactly $10,000, but B's greed really ruined the distribution for the final players.
It's definitely disappointing to walk away with so little. While I understand you feel $10,000 was a fair amount to take, it clearly left very little for those of us at the end of the line.
